title: malus kansuensis var. calva
intro: Malus kansuensis var. calva, also known as the Kansu apple, is a variety of the Malus kansuensis plant, a species of apple native to the mountainous regions of western China. This plant is used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Malus kansuensis var. calva include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate cough and sore throat, and promote wound healing. Malus kansuensis var. calva is often prepared for medicinal use in the form of tea, decoctions, or infusions, and may be combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
